	 Other Names 
	Diluma, Dilu Horse, Dilu, Hex Mark

	 Name Notes 
	Called ‘Hex Mark’ in Koei’s Romance of the Three Kingdoms. According to the novel and notes, Dílú 的廬 means “target skull” and reference spots on the sides of the horse’s head, hence ‘Hex Mark’.

sections.biography-sanguo-yanyi: Dilu was a horse belonging to the bandit Zhang Wu, who rebelled in southern Jing along with Chen Sun. During a battle with Liu Bei, who had been sent to pacify their rebellion, Zhang Wu was run through by Zhao Yun while riding Dilu, and the horse was taken into Liu Bei’s custody. Bei persented the horse to Liu Biao after returning to the capital of Jingzhou.

full_text:
Diluma [Dilu Horse] (Hex Mark Horse) 的廬馬
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Diluma [Dilu Horse] (的廬馬), styledHex Mark Horse. Liu Bei’s horse. Saved Liu Bei at the river Tan. Killed at Fallen Phoenix Slope, along with Pang Tong. Diluma [Dilu Horse] was affiliated with Liu Biao and the Shu Kingdom. Diluma [Dilu Horse] lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

Fact vs Fiction
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
As his original master, Zhang Wu, was fictional, Dilu is probably fictional as well.
Literary Appearances
Romance of the Three Kingdoms:34–35,63,67
BiographySanguo Yanyi
Dilu was a horse belonging to the bandit Zhang Wu, who rebelled in southern Jing along with Chen Sun. During a battle with Liu Bei, who had been sent to pacify their rebellion, Zhang Wu was run through by Zhao Yun while riding Dilu, and the horse was taken into Liu Bei’s custody. Bei persented the horse to Liu Biao after returning to the capital of Jingzhou.
